help_page = r'''import DashboardLayout from "@/components/DashboardLayout";
import { Button } from "@/components/ui/button";
import { trpc } from "@/lib/trpc";
import {
  Activity,
  ArrowRight,
  Bot,
  Brain,
  ExternalLink,
  FileText,
  HelpCircle,
  Loader2,
  MessageSquareText,
  Settings,
  Shield,
  Sparkles,
  TrendingUp,
  Workflow,
} from "lucide-react";
import { useMemo, useState } from "react";
import { useLocation } from "wouter";
import { toast } from "sonner";

type ExplainContext = "overview" | "errors" | "performance";

function HelpCard({
  title,
  description,
  icon,
  onClick,
  cta,
  tone = "default",
}: {
  title: string;
  description: string;
  icon: React.ReactNode;
  onClick: () => void;
  cta: string;
  tone?: "default" | "info" | "success" | "warning";
}) {
  const toneMap = {
    default: "border-border/70 bg-background/30 hover:bg-accent/20",
    info: "border-blue-500/20 bg-blue-500/5 hover:bg-blue-500/10",
    success: "border-emerald-500/20 bg-emerald-500/5 hover:bg-emerald-500/10",
    warning: "border-amber-500/20 bg-amber-500/5 hover:bg-amber-500/10",
  };

  return (
    <button onClick={onClick} className={`w-full text-left rounded-2xl border p-4 transition card-hover ${toneMap[tone]}`}>
      <div className="flex items-start justify-between gap-3 mb-3">
        <div className="p-2 rounded-2xl bg-muted/30 border border-border/50">{icon}</div>
        <ArrowRight className="w-4 h-4 text-muted-foreground shrink-0 mt-1" />
      </div>
      <p className="text-sm font-semibold text-foreground">{title}</p>
      <p className="text-xs text-muted-foreground mt-1 leading-relaxed">{description}</p>
      <p className="text-[11px] text-primary mt-3 font-medium">{cta}</p>
    </button>
  );
}

function SuggestionChip({ label, onClick }: { label: string; onClick: () => void }) {
  return (
    <button onClick={onClick} className="rounded-full border border-border/70 bg-muted/15 px-3 py-1.5 text-xs text-foreground hover:bg-accent/20 transition">
      {label}
    </button>
  );
}

export default function HelpPage() {
  const [, setLocation] = useLocation();
  const [selectedWorkflowId, setSelectedWorkflowId] = useState("");
  const [context, setContext] = useState<ExplainContext>("overview");
  const [assistantPrompt, setAssistantPrompt] = useState("");

  const { data: workflows = [] } = trpc.airtable.workflows.useQuery();
  const explainWorkflow = trpc.intelligence.explainWorkflow.useMutation();

  const selectedWorkflow = useMemo(() => workflows.find((w) => w.recordId === selectedWorkflowId), [workflows, selectedWorkflowId]);

  const runExplain = async () => {
    if (!selectedWorkflow) return;
    await explainWorkflow.mutateAsync({
      workflowId: selectedWorkflow.workflowId,
      workflowName: [selectedWorkflow.name](http://selectedWorkflow.name),
      runtime: selectedWorkflow.runtime,
      status: selectedWorkflow.status,
      context,
    });
  };

  const runAssistantAction = () => {
    const text = assistantPrompt.trim().toLowerCase();
    if (!text) {
      toast.error("Type a request first.");
      return;
    }
    if (text.includes("create") || text.includes("workflow")) return void setLocation("/workflows/new");
    if (text.includes("log") || text.includes("error") || text.includes("failure")) return void setLocation("/logs");
    if (text.includes("ai") || text.includes("prompt") || text.includes("model")) return void setLocation("/ai-logs");
    if (text.includes("performance") || text.includes("campaign") || text.includes("conversion")) return void setLocation("/performance");
    if (text.includes("report") || text.includes("summary")) return void setLocation("/reports");
    if (text.includes("setting") || text.includes("billing") || text.includes("role")) return void setLocation("/settings");
    [toast.info](http://toast.info)("I understood the request, but it is not mapped to an automatic page jump yet.");
  };

  return (
    <DashboardLayout breadcrumbs={[{ label: "Dashboard", path: "/" }, { label: "AI Help" }]}>
      <div className="max-w-[1240px] mx-auto space-y-6">
        <div className="surface-elevated rounded-3xl p-6 md:p-7 overflow-hidden relative">
          <div className="absolute inset-0 pointer-events-none bg-[radial-gradient(circle_at_top_right,rgba(99,102,241,0.12),transparent_28%),radial-gradient(circle_at_left,rgba(16,185,129,0.06),transparent_22%)]" />
          <div className="relative space-y-3">
            <div className="inline-flex items-center gap-2 rounded-full border border-primary/20 bg-primary/10 px-3 py-1.5 text-[11px] font-medium text-primary">
              <Sparkles className="w-3 h-3" />
              AI Product Guide
            </div>
            <div>
              <h1 className="text-heading text-2xl md:text-3xl">AI Help</h1>
              <p className="text-sm text-muted-foreground mt-2 max-w-3xl leading-relaxed">
                This page explains what the platform does, where to go next, and helps you understand workflows without forcing you to learn the whole system at once.
              </p>
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>

        <div className="surface-elevated rounded-2xl p-6 border border-primary/15 glow-primary">
          <div className="flex items-center gap-2 mb-4">
            <div className="p-2 rounded-2xl bg-primary/10 border border-primary/20"><Brain className="w-4 h-4 text-primary" /></div>
            <div>
              <p className="text-sm font-semibold text-foreground">Omni-style assistant</p>
              <p className="text-[11px] text-muted-foreground mt-1">Tell the app what you want to do, and it will route you to the most relevant workspace.</p>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div className="rounded-2xl border border-border/70 bg-background/40 p-4">
            <label className="text-[11px] uppercase tracking-[0.16em] text-muted-foreground font-semibold mb-2 block">Ask for help</label>
            <div className="flex flex-col md:flex-row gap-3">
              <input value={assistantPrompt} onChange={(e) => setAssistantPrompt([e.target](http://e.target).value)} placeholder="Example: show me failed workflows, help me create a new workflow, explain AI logs" className="flex-1 h-11 rounded-xl border border-border bg-background/50 px-3 text-sm text-foreground outline-none" />
              <Button onClick={runAssistantAction} className="h-11 rounded-xl bg-[var(--primary)] text-white hover:opacity-90">
                <MessageSquareText className="w-4 h-4 mr-2" />
                Assist me
              </Button>
            </div>
            <div className="flex flex-wrap gap-2 mt-4">
              <SuggestionChip label="Help me create a workflow" onClick={() => setAssistantPrompt("Help me create a workflow")} />
              <SuggestionChip label="Show me failed workflows" onClick={() => setAssistantPrompt("Show me failed workflows")} />
              <SuggestionChip label="Explain AI logs" onClick={() => setAssistantPrompt("Explain AI logs")} />
              <SuggestionChip label="Take me to reports" onClick={() => setAssistantPrompt("Take me to reports")} />
              <SuggestionChip label="Show performance data" onClick={() => setAssistantPrompt("Show performance data")} />
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>

        <div className="surface-elevated rounded-2xl p-5">
          <div className="flex items-start gap-3">
            <div className="p-2 rounded-2xl bg-primary/10 border border-primary/20 shrink-0"><HelpCircle className="w-4 h-4 text-primary" /></div>
            <div className="min-w-0">
              <p className="text-sm font-semibold text-foreground">What this app is for</p>
              <p className="text-xs text-muted-foreground mt-1 leading-relaxed">
                AgentOps helps a team monitor AI-powered workflows running on Make and n8n, review execution logs, inspect AI decisions, check performance data, and apply governance where needed.
              </p>
              <div className="flex flex-wrap gap-2 mt-4">
                <SuggestionChip label="How do I create a workflow?" onClick={() => setLocation("/workflows/new")} />
                <SuggestionChip label="Where do I see failures?" onClick={() => setLocation("/logs")} />
                <SuggestionChip label="How do I read reports?" onClick={() => setLocation("/reports")} />
                <SuggestionChip label="How do I inspect AI output?" onClick={() => setLocation("/ai-logs")} />
              </div>
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>

        <div className="grid grid-cols-1 xl:grid-cols-3 gap-4">
          <HelpCard title="Create or configure a workflow" description="Use this when you want to start a new automation and choose whether it uses Make or n8n." icon={<Workflow className="w-4 h-4 text-primary" />} onClick={() => setLocation("/workflows/new")} cta="Go to Workflow Setup" tone="info" />
          <HelpCard title="Inspect runtime and execution logs" description="Use logs when a workflow fails or behaves strangely." icon={<Activity className="w-4 h-4 text-blue-400" />} onClick={() => setLocation("/logs")} cta="Open Execution Logs" />
          <HelpCard title="Review AI-generated output" description="Use AI Logs to inspect prompts and model outputs." icon={<Bot className="w-4 h-4 text-purple-400" />} onClick={() => setLocation("/ai-logs")} cta="Open AI Logs" />
          <HelpCard title="Check campaign or business performance" description="Connect workflow activity to campaign results and spend." icon={<TrendingUp className="w-4 h-4 text-emerald-400" />} onClick={() => setLocation("/performance")} cta="Open Performance Data" tone="success" />
          <HelpCard title="Read the final reports" description="Use reports when you want the executive summary and recommendations." icon={<FileText className="w-4 h-4 text-amber-400" />} onClick={() => setLocation("/reports")} cta="Open Reports" tone="warning" />
          <HelpCard title="Manage settings and governance controls" description="Use settings to review billing, access control, and platform configuration." icon={<Settings className="w-4 h-4 text-red-400" />} onClick={() => setLocation("/settings")} cta="Open Settings" />
        </div>

        <div className="surface-elevated rounded-2xl p-6">
          <div className="flex items-center gap-2 mb-4">
            <div className="p-2 rounded-2xl bg-primary/10 border border-primary/20"><Sparkles className="w-4 h-4 text-primary" /></div>
            <div>
              <p className="text-sm font-semibold text-foreground">Explain a workflow for me</p>
              <p className="text-[11px] text-muted-foreground mt-1">This uses the existing AI explainer to make a workflow easier to understand.</p>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div className="grid grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-[1.2fr_0.9fr_auto] gap-3 mb-4">
            <div>
              <label className="text-[11px] uppercase tracking-[0.16em] text-muted-foreground font-semibold mb-1.5 block">Workflow</label>
              <select value={selectedWorkflowId} onChange={(e) => setSelectedWorkflowId([e.target](http://e.target).value)} className="w-full h-10 rounded-xl border border-border bg-background/50 text-sm px-3 text-foreground outline-none">
                <option value="">Select a workflow…</option>
                {[workflows.map](http://workflows.map)((wf) => (
                  <option key={wf.recordId} value={wf.recordId}>
                    {wf.workflowId} — {[wf.name](http://wf.name)}
                  </option>
                ))}
              </select>
            </div>
            <div>
              <label className="text-[11px] uppercase tracking-[0.16em] text-muted-foreground font-semibold mb-1.5 block">Explain focus</label>
              <select value={context} onChange={(e) => setContext([e.target](http://e.target).value as ExplainContext)} className="w-full h-10 rounded-xl border border-border bg-background/50 text-sm px-3 text-foreground outline-none">
                <option value="overview">Overview</option>
                <option value="errors">Errors</option>
                <option value="performance">Performance</option>
              </select>
            </div>
            <div className="flex items-end">
              <Button onClick={runExplain} disabled={!selectedWorkflow || explainWorkflow.isPending} className="h-10 rounded-xl bg-[var(--primary)] text-white hover:opacity-90">
                {explainWorkflow.isPending ? (
                  <>
                    <Loader2 className="w-4 h-4 mr-2 animate-spin" />
                    Explaining…
                  </>
                ) : (
                  <>
                    <Sparkles className="w-4 h-4 mr-2" />
                    Explain
                  </>
                )}
              </Button>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div className="rounded-2xl border border-border/70 bg-muted/10 p-4 min-h-[140px]">
            {!selectedWorkflow && ![explainWorkflow.data](http://explainWorkflow.data) && (
              <div className="text-center py-6">
                <p className="text-sm font-medium text-foreground">Select a workflow to begin</p>
                <p className="text-xs text-muted-foreground mt-1">The assistant will explain what it does, what may be wrong, or how to read its performance.</p>
              </div>
            )}
            {selectedWorkflow && ![explainWorkflow.data](http://explainWorkflow.data) && !explainWorkflow.isPending && (
              <div className="text-left">
                <p className="text-xs font-semibold text-foreground mb-2">Ready to explain:</p>
                <div className="flex items-center gap-2 flex-wrap">
                  <span className="code-inline">{selectedWorkflow.workflowId}</span>
                  <span className="text-xs text-muted-foreground">{[selectedWorkflow.name](http://selectedWorkflow.name)}</span>
                  <span className="text-xs text-muted-foreground">· {selectedWorkflow.runtime}</span>
                  <span className="text-xs text-muted-foreground">· {selectedWorkflow.status}</span>
                </div>
              </div>
            )}
            {[explainWorkflow.data](http://explainWorkflow.data) && (
              <div>
                <p className="text-xs font-semibold text-foreground mb-2">AI explanation</p>
                <p className="text-sm text-foreground/90 leading-relaxed whitespace-pre-wrap">
                  {typeof [explainWorkflow.data](http://explainWorkflow.data).explanation === "string" ? [explainWorkflow.data](http://explainWorkflow.data).explanation : JSON.stringify([explainWorkflow.data](http://explainWorkflow.data).explanation, null, 2)}
                </p>
              </div>
            )}
          </div>
        </div>

        <div className="surface-elevated rounded-2xl p-5">
          <div className="flex items-center gap-2 mb-3">
            <Shield className="w-4 h-4 text-emerald-400" />
            <p className="text-sm font-semibold text-foreground">Suggested first path for a new user</p>
          </div>

          <div className="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-4 gap-3">
            <div className="rounded-2xl border border-border/70 bg-background/30 p-4">
              <p className="text-xs font-semibold text-foreground mb-1">Step 1</p>
              <p className="text-[11px] text-muted-foreground leading-relaxed">Start on the dashboard and review the current workflows and alerts.</p>
            </div>
            <div className="rounded-2xl border border-border/70 bg-background/30 p-4">
              <p className="text-xs font-semibold text-foreground mb-1">Step 2</p>
              <p className="text-[11px] text-muted-foreground leading-relaxed">Open the execution logs to understand what the system records.</p>
            </div>
            <div className="rounded-2xl border border-border/70 bg-background/30 p-4">
              <p className="text-xs font-semibold text-foreground mb-1">Step 3</p>
              <p className="text-[11px] text-muted-foreground leading-relaxed">Inspect AI Logs to see how prompts and responses are captured.</p>
            </div>
            <div className="rounded-2xl border border-primary/30 bg-primary/10 p-4">
              <p className="text-xs font-semibold text-foreground mb-1">Step 4</p>
              <p className="text-[11px] text-muted-foreground leading-relaxed">Create or configure a workflow when you are ready to operate.</p>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div className="flex flex-wrap gap-2 mt-4">
            <Button onClick={() => setLocation("/workflows/new")} className="rounded-xl bg-[var(--primary)] text-white hover:opacity-90">
              <Workflow className="w-4 h-4 mr-2" />
              Create Workflow
            </Button>
            <Button variant="outline" onClick={() => setLocation("/logs")} className="rounded-xl bg-transparent">
              <ExternalLink className="w-4 h-4 mr-2" />
              View Logs
            </Button>
          </div>
        </div>
      </div>
    </DashboardLayout>
  );
}
'''
app = r'''import HomePage from "./pages/HomePage";
import AuthPanel from "./components/AuthPanel";
import { Toaster } from "@/components/ui/sonner";
import { TooltipProvider } from "@/components/ui/tooltip";
import { useTheme } from "@/contexts/ThemeContext";
import NotFound from "@/pages/NotFound";
import { Route, Switch } from "wouter";
import ErrorBoundary from "./components/ErrorBoundary";
import { ThemeProvider } from "./contexts/ThemeContext";
import Dashboard from "./pages/Dashboard";
import WorkflowNew from "./pages/WorkflowNew";
import WorkflowDetail from "./pages/WorkflowDetail";
import ReportsPage from "./pages/ReportsPage";
import WebhookSimulator from "./pages/WebhookSimulator";
import PerformanceData from "./pages/PerformanceData";
import SettingsPage from "./pages/SettingsPage";
import SystemLogsPage from "./pages/SystemLogsPage";
import ExecutionLogsPage from "./pages/ExecutionLogsPage";
import AILogsPage from "./pages/AILogsPage";
import WorkflowConfig from "./pages/WorkflowConfig";
import HelpPage from "./pages/HelpPage";

function Router() {
  return (
    <Switch>
      <Route path="/home" component={HomePage} />
      <Route path="/signin" component={AuthPanel} />
      <Route path="/" component={Dashboard} />
      <Route path="/workflows/new" component={WorkflowNew} />
      <Route path="/workflows/:id" component={WorkflowDetail} />
      <Route path="/reports" component={ReportsPage} />
      <Route path="/webhooks" component={WebhookSimulator} />
      <Route path="/performance" component={PerformanceData} />
      <Route path="/settings" component={SettingsPage} />
      <Route path="/system-logs" component={SystemLogsPage} />
      <Route path="/logs" component={ExecutionLogsPage} />
      <Route path="/ai-logs" component={AILogsPage} />
      <Route path="/workflows/:id/config" component={WorkflowConfig} />
      <Route path="/help" component={HelpPage} />
      <Route path="/404" component={NotFound} />
      <Route component={NotFound} />
    </Switch>
  );
}

function AppShell() {
  const { theme } = useTheme();
  const isDark = theme === "dark";

  return (
    <TooltipProvider>
      <Toaster
        theme={isDark ? "dark" : "light"}
        toastOptions={{
          style: {
            background: isDark ? "oklch(0.13 0.018 250)" : "oklch(0.99 0.002 250)",
            border: isDark ? "1px solid oklch(0.22 0.015 250)" : "1px solid oklch(0.9 0.01 250)",
            color: isDark ? "oklch(0.93 0.01 250)" : "oklch(0.2 0.02 250)",
          },
        }}
      />
      <Router />
    </TooltipProvider>
  );
}

function App() {
  return (
    <ErrorBoundary>
      <ThemeProvider defaultTheme="dark" switchable>
        <AppShell />
      </ThemeProvider>
    </ErrorBoundary>
  );
}

export default App;

dashboard_layout = r'''import { useAuth } from "@/_core/hooks/useAuth";
import AuthPanel from "@/components/AuthPanel";

const NAV_GROUPS = [
  {

      { icon: CreditCard, label: "Plan & Billing", path: "/settings" },
    ],
  },

const SIDEBAR_WIDTH_KEY = "agentops-sidebar-width";
const DEFAULT_WIDTH = 248;
const MIN_WIDTH = 220;
const MAX_WIDTH = 360;

function useSystemStatus() {
  const [status, setStatus] = useState<"live" | "syncing" | "error">("live");
  const [lastSync, setLastSync] = useState<Date>(new Date());

  useEffect(() => {
    const interval = setInterval(() => {
      setStatus("syncing");
      setTimeout(() => {
        setStatus("live");
        setLastSync(new Date());
      }, 1200);
    }, 45000);
    return () => clearInterval(interval);
  }, []);

  return { status, lastSync };
}

function Topbar({ breadcrumbs }: { breadcrumbs?: { label: string; path?: string }[] }) {
  const { theme, toggleTheme } = useTheme();
  const { status, lastSync } = useSystemStatus();
  const { user, logout } = useAuth();
  const [, setLocation] = useLocation();
  const { toggleSidebar } = useSidebar();

  const statusConfig = {
    live: { label: "Live", color: "text-emerald-400", dot: "bg-emerald-400" },
    syncing: { label: "Syncing…", color: "text-blue-400", dot: "bg-blue-400" },
    error: { label: "Error", color: "text-red-400", dot: "bg-red-400" },
  }[status];

  const formatLastSync = (d: Date) => {
    const diff = Math.floor(([Date.now](http://Date.now)() - d.getTime()) / 1000);
    if (diff < 60) return `${diff}s ago`;
    return `${Math.floor(diff / 60)}m ago`;
  };

  return (
    <header className="sticky top-0 z-40 flex items-center justify-between gap-4 border-b border-border/60 bg-background/80 px-5 py-3 backdrop-blur-xl">
      <div className="flex items-center gap-3 min-w-0">
        <button onClick={toggleSidebar} className="h-8 w-8 flex items-center justify-center rounded-xl border border-border/60 bg-background/70 hover:bg-accent transition-colors shrink-0" aria-label="Toggle sidebar">
          <PanelLeft className="h-3.5 w-3.5 text-muted-foreground" />
        </button>

        {breadcrumbs && breadcrumbs.length > 0 && (
          <nav className="flex items-center gap-1.5 text-xs text-muted-foreground min-w-0">
            <button onClick={() => setLocation("/home")} className="text-muted-foreground/70 hover:text-foreground transition-colors font-medium">
              AgentOps
            </button>
            {[breadcrumbs.map](http://breadcrumbs.map)((crumb, i) => (
              <span key={i} className="flex items-center gap-1.5 min-w-0">
                <ChevronRight className="w-3 h-3 shrink-0 text-muted-foreground/40" />
                {crumb.path ? (
                  <button onClick={() => setLocation(crumb.path!)} className="hover:text-foreground transition-colors truncate">
                    {crumb.label}
                  </button>
                ) : (
                  <span className="text-foreground font-medium truncate">{crumb.label}</span>
                )}
              </span>
            ))}
          </nav>
        )}
      </div>

      <div className="flex items-center gap-2 shrink-0">
        <Tooltip>
          <TooltipTrigger asChild>
            <div className="flex items-center gap-1.5 rounded-full border border-border/60 bg-background/70 px-3 py-1.5 cursor-default select-none shadow-sm">
              <span className={`w-2 h-2 rounded-full ${[statusConfig.dot](http://statusConfig.dot)}`} />
              <span className={`text-xs font-medium ${statusConfig.color}`}>{statusConfig.label}</span>
            </div>
          </TooltipTrigger>
          <TooltipContent side="bottom" className="text-xs">
            <p className="font-medium">Workspace sync status</p>
            <p className="text-muted-foreground mt-0.5">Last synced {formatLastSync(lastSync)}</p>
          </TooltipContent>
        </Tooltip>

        <Tooltip>
          <TooltipTrigger asChild>
            <Button variant="ghost" size="icon" className="h-8 w-8 rounded-xl border border-border/60 bg-background/70" onClick={toggleTheme} aria-label="Toggle theme">
              {theme === "dark" ? <Sun className="h-3.5 w-3.5 text-muted-foreground" /> : <Moon className="h-3.5 w-3.5 text-muted-foreground" />}
            </Button>
          </TooltipTrigger>
          <TooltipContent side="bottom" className="text-xs">
            Switch to {theme === "dark" ? "light" : "dark"} mode
          </TooltipContent>
        </Tooltip>

        <DropdownMenu>
          <DropdownMenuTrigger asChild>
            <button className="flex items-center gap-2 rounded-xl border border-border/60 bg-background/70 px-2.5 py-1.5 hover:bg-accent/60 transition-colors focus:outline-none shadow-sm">
              <Avatar className="h-7 w-7 border border-border/70">
                <AvatarFallback className="text-[10px] font-semibold bg-primary/15 text-primary">
                  {user?.name?.charAt(0).toUpperCase() ?? "U"}
                </AvatarFallback>
              </Avatar>
              <span className="text-xs font-medium text-foreground hidden sm:block max-w-[120px] truncate">{user?.name ?? "User"}</span>
            </button>
          </DropdownMenuTrigger>

          <DropdownMenuContent align="end" className="w-56 rounded-xl">
            <DropdownMenuLabel className="font-normal">
              <div className="flex flex-col gap-0.5">
                <p className="text-sm font-medium">{user?.name ?? "User"}</p>
                <p className="text-xs text-muted-foreground truncate">{user?.email ?? ""}</p>
                <Badge variant="secondary" className="w-fit mt-1 text-[10px] px-1.5 py-0 rounded-full">
                  {user?.role === "admin" ? "Admin" : user?.role === "analyst" ? "Analyst" : "Viewer"}
                </Badge>
              </div>
            </DropdownMenuLabel>
            <DropdownMenuSeparator />
            <DropdownMenuItem onClick={() => setLocation("/home")} className="cursor-pointer text-xs">
              <Home className="mr-2 h-3.5 w-3.5" />
              Homepage
            </DropdownMenuItem>
            <DropdownMenuItem onClick={() => setLocation("/help")} className="cursor-pointer text-xs">
              <HelpCircle className="mr-2 h-3.5 w-3.5" />
              AI Help
            </DropdownMenuItem>
            <DropdownMenuItem onClick={() => setLocation("/settings")} className="cursor-pointer text-xs">
              <CreditCard className="mr-2 h-3.5 w-3.5" />
              Plan & Billing
            </DropdownMenuItem>
            <DropdownMenuItem className="cursor-pointer text-xs opacity-50" onClick={() => {}}>
              <Settings className="mr-2 h-3.5 w-3.5" />
              Settings
            </DropdownMenuItem>
            <DropdownMenuSeparator />
            <DropdownMenuItem onClick={logout} className="cursor-pointer text-xs text-destructive focus:text-destructive">
              <LogOut className="mr-2 h-3.5 w-3.5" />
              Sign out
            </DropdownMenuItem>
          </DropdownMenuContent>
        </DropdownMenu>
      </div>
    </header>
  );
}

function AppSidebar() {
  const [location, setLocation] = useLocation();
  const { state } = useSidebar();
  const isCollapsed = state === "collapsed";
  const { data: stats } = trpc.airtable.dashboardStats.useQuery(undefined, { refetchInterval: 60000 });

  return (
    <Sidebar collapsible="icon" className="border-r border-sidebar-border bg-sidebar/90 backdrop-blur-xl">
      <SidebarHeader className="border-b border-sidebar-border/70 px-3 py-3">
        <button onClick={() => setLocation("/home")} className="flex items-center gap-2.5 h-full text-left w-full">
          <div className="w-8 h-8 rounded-2xl bg-primary/15 ring-1 ring-primary/20 flex items-center justify-center shrink-0 shadow-sm">
            <Shield className="w-4 h-4 text-primary" />
          </div>
          {!isCollapsed && (
            <div className="min-w-0">
              <p className="text-sm font-semibold tracking-tight truncate">AgentOps</p>
              <p className="text-[10px] text-muted-foreground truncate">Enterprise agent governance</p>
            </div>
          )}
        </button>
      </SidebarHeader>

      <SidebarContent className="gap-0 pt-3 pb-3">
        {!isCollapsed && (
          <div className="mx-3 mb-4 rounded-2xl border border-border/70 bg-gradient-to-br from-primary/12 via-primary/6 to-transparent p-4 shadow-sm">
            <div className="flex items-start justify-between gap-3">
              <div>
                <p className="text-xs font-semibold text-foreground">Operations Command</p>
                <p className="mt-1 text-[11px] leading-relaxed text-muted-foreground">
                  Monitor autonomous agents, intervene on exceptions, and keep every workflow audit-ready.
                </p>
              </div>
              <Sparkles className="w-4 h-4 text-primary shrink-0 mt-0.5" />
            </div>
          </div>
        )}

        {NAV_[GROUPS.map](http://GROUPS.map)((group) => (
          <div key={group.label} className="mb-2">
            {!isCollapsed && (
              <p className="px-4 py-1.5 text-[10px] font-semibold uppercase tracking-[0.16em] text-muted-foreground/60">
                {group.label}
              </p>
            )}
            <SidebarMenu className="px-2">
              {[group.items.map](http://group.items.map)((item) => {
                const isActive = item.path === "/" ? location === "/" : location.startsWith(item.path);
                const isPrimaryAction = item.label === "New Workflow";
                return (
                  <SidebarMenuItem key={item.path}>
                    <SidebarMenuButton isActive={isActive} onClick={() => setLocation(item.path)} className={`h-9 rounded-xl px-3 ${isPrimaryAction ? "bg-primary text-white hover:opacity-90" : ""}`}>
                      <item.icon className={`h-4 w-4 shrink-0 ${isActive ? (isPrimaryAction ? "text-white" : "text-primary") : isPrimaryAction ? "text-white" : "text-muted-foreground"}`} />
                      <span className={isPrimaryAction ? "text-white font-medium" : isActive ? "text-foreground font-medium" : "text-muted-foreground"}>
                        {item.label}
                      </span>
                    </SidebarMenuButton>
                  </SidebarMenuItem>
                );
              })}
            </SidebarMenu>
          </div>
        ))}

        {!isCollapsed && (
          <div className="mx-3 mt-2 p-4 rounded-2xl bg-background/70 border border-border/70 shadow-sm">
            <p className="text-[10px] font-semibold uppercase tracking-[0.16em] text-muted-foreground/70 mb-3">Runtime Footprint</p>
            <div className="space-y-2.5">
              <div className="flex items-center justify-between rounded-xl border border-border/60 bg-muted/20 px-3 py-2">
                <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
                  <Zap className="w-3 h-3 text-violet-400" />
                  <span className="text-xs text-foreground">Make</span>
                </div>
                <span className="text-xs font-semibold text-foreground">{stats?.make ?? "—"}</span>
              </div>
              <div className="flex items-center justify-between rounded-xl border border-border/60 bg-muted/20 px-3 py-2">
                <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
                  <Zap className="w-3 h-3 text-orange-400" />
                  <span className="text-xs text-foreground">n8n</span>
                </div>
                <span className="text-xs font-semibold text-foreground">{stats?.n8n ?? "—"}</span>
              </div>
            </div>
          </div>
        )}
      </SidebarContent>

      <SidebarFooter className="p-3 border-t border-sidebar-border/70">
        {!isCollapsed ? (
          <button onClick={() => setLocation("/help")} className="w-full rounded-2xl border border-border/70 bg-gradient-to-br from-background to-muted/30 px-4 py-3 text-left hover:border-primary/30 hover:bg-accent/40 transition-colors shadow-sm">
            <div className="flex items-start justify-between gap-3">
              <div>
                <p className="text-xs font-semibold text-foreground">Need help?</p>
                <p className="mt-1 text-[11px] text-muted-foreground leading-relaxed">
                  Use AI Help to understand the app, workflows, and where to go next.
                </p>
              </div>
              <HelpCircle className="w-4 h-4 text-muted-foreground shrink-0 mt-0.5" />
            </div>
          </button>
        ) : (
          <SidebarMenu>
            <SidebarMenuItem>
              <SidebarMenuButton tooltip="AI Help" onClick={() => setLocation("/help")} className="h-9 rounded-xl">
                <HelpCircle className="h-4 w-4 text-muted-foreground" />
                <span className="text-muted-foreground">AI Help</span>
              </SidebarMenuButton>
            </SidebarMenuItem>
          </SidebarMenu>
        )}
      </SidebarFooter>
    </Sidebar>
  );
}

export interface DashboardLayoutProps {
  children: React.ReactNode;
  breadcrumbs?: { label: string; path?: string }[];
}

export default function DashboardLayout({ children, breadcrumbs }: DashboardLayoutProps) {
  const [sidebarWidth, setSidebarWidth] = useState(() => {
    try {
      const saved = localStorage.getItem(SIDEBAR_WIDTH_KEY);
      return saved ? parseInt(saved, 10) : DEFAULT_WIDTH;
    } catch {
      return DEFAULT_WIDTH;
    }
  });

  const { loading, user } = useAuth();

  useEffect(() => {
    try {
      localStorage.setItem(SIDEBAR_WIDTH_KEY, sidebarWidth.toString());
    } catch {}
  }, [sidebarWidth]);

  if (loading) return <DashboardLayoutSkeleton />;
  if (!user) return <AuthPanel />;

  return (
    <SidebarProvider style={{ "--sidebar-width": `${sidebarWidth}px` } as CSSProperties}>
      <LayoutContent setSidebarWidth={setSidebarWidth} breadcrumbs={breadcrumbs}>
        {children}
      </LayoutContent>
    </SidebarProvider>
  );
}

function LayoutContent({
  children,
  setSidebarWidth,
  breadcrumbs,
}: {
  children: React.ReactNode;
  setSidebarWidth: (w: number) => void;
  breadcrumbs?: { label: string; path?: string }[];
}) {
  const { state } = useSidebar();
  const isCollapsed = state === "collapsed";
  const [isResizing, setIsResizing] = useState(false);
  const sidebarRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);
  const isMobile = useIsMobile();
  const [location, setLocation] = useLocation();

  useEffect(() => {
    if (!["/signin", "/home"].includes(location)) {
      sessionStorage.setItem("agentops:last-path", location);
    }
  }, [location]);

  const autoBreadcrumbs =
    breadcrumbs ??
    (() => {
      const map: Record<string, string> = {
        "/home": "Homepage",
        "/": "Dashboard",
        "/workflows/new": "New Workflow",
        "/reports": "Reports",
        "/webhooks": "Webhook Simulator",
        "/performance": "Performance Data",
        "/settings": "Plan & Billing",
        "/system-logs": "System Logs",
        "/logs": "Execution Logs",
        "/ai-logs": "AI Logs",
        "/help": "AI Help",
      };
      const label = map[location];
      if (!label) {
        if (location.startsWith("/workflows/")) {
          return [
            { label: "Dashboard", path: "/" },
            { label: "Workflow Detail" },
          ];
        }
        return [];
      }
      return [{ label }];
    })();

  useEffect(() => {
    if (isCollapsed) setIsResizing(false);
  }, [isCollapsed]);

  useEffect(() => {
    const handleMouseMove = (e: MouseEvent) => {
      if (!isResizing) return;
      const left = sidebarRef.current?.getBoundingClientRect().left ?? 0;
      const newWidth = e.clientX - left;
      if (newWidth >= MIN_WIDTH && newWidth <= MAX_WIDTH) setSidebarWidth(newWidth);
    };

    const handleMouseUp = () => setIsResizing(false);

    if (isResizing) {
      document.addEventListener("mousemove", handleMouseMove);
      document.addEventListener("mouseup", handleMouseUp);
      [document.body.style](http://document.body.style).cursor = "col-resize";
      [document.body.style](http://document.body.style).userSelect = "none";
    }

    return () => {
      document.removeEventListener("mousemove", handleMouseMove);
      document.removeEventListener("mouseup", handleMouseUp);
      [document.body.style](http://document.body.style).cursor = "";
      [document.body.style](http://document.body.style).userSelect = "";
    };
  }, [isResizing, setSidebarWidth]);

  return (
    <>
      <div className="relative" ref={sidebarRef}>
        <AppSidebar />
        {!isCollapsed && !isMobile && (
          <div className="absolute top-0 right-0 w-1 h-full cursor-col-resize hover:bg-primary/20 transition-colors" style={{ zIndex: 50 }} onMouseDown={() => setIsResizing(true)} />
        )}
      </div>

      <SidebarInset className="flex flex-col min-h-screen bg-[radial-gradient(circle_at_top,rgba(99,102,241,0.06),transparent_28%),radial-gradient(circle_at_top_right,rgba(16,185,129,0.04),transparent_18%)]">
        {isMobile && (
          <div className="flex border-b border-border/60 h-12 items-center justify-between bg-background/90 px-3 backdrop-blur sticky top-0 z-40">
            <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
              <SidebarTrigger className="h-7 w-7 rounded-md" />
              <button onClick={() => setLocation("/home")} className="text-sm font-medium">
                AgentOps
              </button>
            </div>
          </div>
        )}
        {!isMobile && <Topbar breadcrumbs={autoBreadcrumbs} />}
        <main className="flex-1 px-6 py-6 animate-in">{children}</main>

        <button
          onClick={() => setLocation("/help")}
          className="fixed bottom-6 right-6 z-50 inline-flex items-center gap-2 rounded-full bg-[var(--primary)] text-white px-4 py-3 shadow-lg hover:opacity-90 transition-all border border-white/10"
          aria-label="Open AI Help"
          title="AI Help"
        >
          <Sparkles className="w-4 h-4" />
          <span className="text-xs font-medium hidden sm:inline">AI Help</span>
        </button>
      </SidebarInset>
    </>
  );
}
'''
(base/'pages'/'Dashboard.tsx').write_text(dashboard, encoding='utf-8')
(base/'components'/'DashboardLayout.tsx').write_text(dashboard_layout, encoding='utf-8')
(base/'components'/'AuthPanel.tsx').write_text(auth, encoding='utf-8')
(base/'pages'/'HomePage.tsx').write_text(home, encoding='utf-8')
(base/'pages'/'HelpPage.tsx').write_text(help_page, encoding='utf-8')
(base/'App.tsx').write_text(app, encoding='utf-8')

    for p in base.rglob('*'):
        if [p.is](http://p.is)_file():
            z.write(p, p.relative_to(base.parent))
print(f"Created {zip_path}")
print("Files:")
for p in sorted(base.rglob('*')):
    if [p.is](http://p.is)_file():
        print(p)

import { useAuth } from "@/_core/hooks/useAuth";
import DashboardLayout from "@/components/DashboardLayout";
import { Button } from "@/components/ui/button";
import { Plus, RefreshCw } from "lucide-react";
import { trpc } from "@/lib/trpc";
import { useLocation } from "wouter";
import { useState } from "react";

export default function Dashboard() {
  const [, setLocation] = useLocation();
  const utils = trpc.useUtils();
  const { user } = useAuth();
  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);

  const refresh = async () => {
    setLoading(true);
    await utils.airtable.dashboardStats.invalidate();
    await utils.airtable.workflows.invalidate();
    setLoading(false);
  };

  return (
    <DashboardLayout breadcrumbs={[{ label: "Dashboard" }]}>
      <div className="max-w-[1200px] mx-auto space-y-6">

        {/* HEADER */}
        <div className="rounded-2xl border p-6 bg-background/60">
          <h1 className="text-2xl font-semibold">AgentOps Dashboard</h1>
          <p className="text-sm text-muted-foreground mt-2">
            Monitor workflows, AI logs, and performance in one place.
          </p>

          <div className="flex gap-3 mt-4">
            <Button
              onClick={() => setLocation("/workflows/new")}
              className="bg-primary text-white"
            >
              <Plus className="w-4 h-4 mr-2" />
              New Workflow
            </Button>

            <Button variant="outline" onClick={refresh}>
              <RefreshCw className="w-4 h-4 mr-2" />
              {loading ? "Refreshing..." : "Refresh"}
            </Button>
          </div>
        </div>

        {/* QUICK NAV */}
        <div className="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 gap-4">

          <div
            onClick={() => setLocation("/logs")}
            className="cursor-pointer rounded-2xl border p-5 hover:bg-accent transition"
          >
            <h3 className="font-semibold">Execution Logs</h3>
            <p className="text-xs text-muted-foreground mt-2">
              View workflow runs and failures
            </p>
          </div>

          <div
            onClick={() => setLocation("/ai-logs")}
            className="cursor-pointer rounded-2xl border p-5 hover:bg-accent transition"
          >
            <h3 className="font-semibold">AI Logs</h3>
            <p className="text-xs text-muted-foreground mt-2">
              Inspect prompts and responses
            </p>
          </div>

          <div
            onClick={() => setLocation("/reports")}
            className="cursor-pointer rounded-2xl border p-5 hover:bg-accent transition"
          >
            <h3 className="font-semibold">Reports</h3>
            <p className="text-xs text-muted-foreground mt-2">
              Insights and summaries
            </p>
          </div>

        </div>

        {/* WELCOME */}
        <div className="rounded-2xl border p-5 bg-blue-500/5">
          <p className="text-sm font-semibold">Quick Start</p>
          <p className="text-xs text-muted-foreground mt-1">
            1. Create workflow → 2. Check logs → 3. View reports
          </p>
        </div>

      </div>
    </DashboardLayout>
  );
}

🟢 3. App.tsx (ROUTING FIX)
Replace your App.tsx with:

import { Route, Switch } from "wouter";
import Dashboard from "./pages/Dashboard";
import HomePage from "./pages/HomePage";
import HelpPage from "./pages/HelpPage";
import AuthPanel from "./components/AuthPanel";

export default function App() {
  return (
    <Switch>
      <Route path="/home" component={HomePage} />
      <Route path="/help" component={HelpPage} />
      <Route path="/signin" component={AuthPanel} />
      <Route path="/" component={Dashboard} />
    </Switch>
  );
}

const [sidebarWidth, setSidebarWidth] = useState(() => {
  try {
    const saved = localStorage.getItem(SIDEBAR_WIDTH_KEY);
    return saved ? parseInt(saved, 10) : DEFAULT_WIDTH;
  } catch {
    return DEFAULT_WIDTH;
  }
});

const [sidebarOpen, setSidebarOpen] = useState(() => {
  try {
    const saved = localStorage.getItem(SIDEBAR_OPEN_KEY);
    return saved === null ? true : saved === "true";
  } catch {
    return true;
  }
});
Then replace the existing width-only useEffect at lines 340–344 with:
useEffect(() => {
  try {
    localStorage.setItem(SIDEBAR_WIDTH_KEY, sidebarWidth.toString());
  } catch {}
}, [sidebarWidth]);

useEffect(() => {
  try {
    localStorage.setItem(SIDEBAR_OPEN_KEY, String(sidebarOpen));
  } catch {}
}, [sidebarOpen]);
Then replace the SidebarProvider opening tag at lines 349–354 with:
<SidebarProvider
  open={sidebarOpen}
  onOpenChange={setSidebarOpen}
  style={{ "--sidebar-width": `${sidebarWidth}px` } as CSSProperties}
>
  <LayoutContent setSidebarWidth={setSidebarWidth} breadcrumbs={breadcrumbs}>
    {children}
  </LayoutContent>
</SidebarProvider>
This is the exact place where the persistence change belongs.

Current repo status
App.tsx is mostly okay
Routes for /home, /signin, and /help are present. However, / appears before some more specific routes, which can sometimes cause routing issues depending on how wouter resolves matches. Safer order is: specific routes first, / later. 


DashboardLayout.tsx is broken
Several changes were pasted into the wrong place:

SidebarProvider logic was inserted inside AppSidebar(), which is incorrect.

children and breadcrumbs are referenced inside AppSidebar() where they do not exist.

There is a literal 0</DropdownMenuItem> typo in the sign-out item.

The floating AI Help button contains another nested <button> inside it, which is invalid JSX.

Sidebar open-state persistence was added in the wrong component; it belongs in DashboardLayout, not AppSidebar.

These issues are in the current live GitHub file. 


Dashboard.tsx is also broken
The AccessPanel and AdminControlPanel changes were inserted incorrectly:

setLocation is used inside AccessPanel, but that component does not define it.

analyst and admin are referenced inside AccessPanel, but those variables do not exist there.

The AccessPanel JSX structure is malformed with extra closing </div>.

AdminControlPanel uses setLocation without defining it.

Admin cards were pasted inside the header area instead of the grid section.

Some cards are duplicated.

This will fail TypeScript/JSX build.

create table if not exists ui_resources (
  id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  resource_key text not null unique,
  resource_group text not null,
  resource_type text not null default 'text',
  value jsonb not null,
  is_active boolean not null default true,
  created_at timestamptz not null default now(),
  updated_at timestamptz not null default now()
);

Use this in Claude Code:

You are working on my GitHub repo: dkDesign254/agentops-platform.

Project context:
AgentOps is an AI agent operations and governance platform for monitoring, managing, and understanding workflows/agents running across Make and n8n. It connects Airtable/Supabase data, workflow logs, AI logs, performance reports, and a named assistant called GAIA AI.

Current state:
- The app is live on Render: https://agentops-platform.onrender.com
- Supabase is connected.
- Airtable data is feeding the dashboard.
- The currently active dashboard is DashboardFixed.tsx.
- Dashboard.tsx is legacy/corrupted and should be carefully replaced only after confirming the fixed dashboard builds cleanly.
- DashboardLayout.tsx has the sidebar/topbar, runtime footprint, and floating GAIA AI button.
- HelpPage.tsx is GAIA AI.
- The app currently routes to DashboardFixed.tsx from App.tsx.

Your goals, in order:

1. Inspect the repo first.
   - Identify current routing in client/src/App.tsx.
   - Review:
     - client/src/pages/DashboardFixed.tsx
     - client/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx
     - client/src/components/DashboardLayout.tsx
     - client/src/pages/HelpPage.tsx
     - client/src/pages/HomePage.tsx
     - client/src/components/AuthPanel.tsx
     - client/src/index.css or global CSS/theme files
     - server/trpc routers and schema files related to Airtable, Supabase, auth, intelligence.

2. Stabilise architecture.
   - Confirm DashboardFixed.tsx builds and works.
   - Replace the broken legacy Dashboard.tsx with the clean contents of DashboardFixed.tsx.
   - Update App.tsx to import from "./pages/Dashboard" instead of "./pages/DashboardFixed".
   - Delete DashboardFixed.tsx only after confirming no imports depend on it.
   - Run typecheck/build after this.

3. Standardise GAIA AI naming.
   Replace all user-facing uses of:
   - “AI Help”
   - “Omni-style assistant”
   - “Operator AI”
   with:
   - “GAIA AI”
   - “Ask GAIA”
   - “Open GAIA”
   - “GAIA explanation”
   where contextually appropriate.

   Do not rename technical route /help unless routing is updated safely. The route can remain /help, but labels should say GAIA AI.

4. Fix UI/UX contrast and polish.
   Priority issues:
   - Buttons must be visible in both light and dark mode.
   - New Workflow must look like a clear primary CTA.
   - Refresh button must not be washed out.
   - Floating GAIA AI button must be visible in light and dark mode.
   - Sidebar collapsed state must persist until manually changed.
   - Runtime Footprint Make/n8n buttons must be clickable and route to filtered workflow/log views.
   - Any card that looks clickable must actually navigate.
   - Top-right user menu should include:
     - Homepage
     - GAIA AI
     - Workspace Settings
     - Plan & Billing
     - System Health
     - Sign out

5. Make runtime drill-down actually useful.
   Current runtime links use:
   - /logs?runtime=make
   - /logs?runtime=n8n

   Inspect ExecutionLogsPage or logs page.
   If it does not read query params yet, implement runtime/status query parameter support:
   - runtime=make
   - runtime=n8n
   - status=running
   - status=pending
   - status=failed

   Filters should pre-populate based on URL params and allow the user to clear or change them.

6. Build Android-style resource separation.
   The codebase currently has too much hardcoded UI content. Implement a resource layer.

   Preferred architecture:
   - Supabase table for dynamic UI resources
   - local fallback JSON/TS resource file for safe defaults

   Create or propose SQL migration for:
   ui_resources:
   - id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid()
   - resource_key text unique not null
   - resource_group text not null
   - resource_type text not null default 'text'
   - value jsonb not null
   - is_active boolean not null default true
   - created_at timestamptz not null default now()
   - updated_at timestamptz not null default now()

   Add seed resources for:
   - homepage hero title
   - homepage hero description
   - homepage feature cards
   - dashboard quick start text
   - GAIA AI title and description
   - sidebar labels if practical
   - onboarding guidance

   Implement:
   - client fallback resources
   - server endpoint/trpc method to fetch active resources
   - client hook/useResources or equivalent
   - keep fallback if Supabase/resource fetch fails

   Do not break the current UI if Supabase resources are unavailable.

7. Improve Homepage and Sign-in page.
   Home/sign-in should feel like a warm enterprise SaaS landing experience inspired by Airtable/Softr, not a basic login page.
   Requirements:
   - clear hero section
   - explanation of what AgentOps does
   - sections for Monitor, Understand, Improve
   - clear “Enter workspace” CTA
   - clear “Ask GAIA” CTA
   - feature cards linking into app sections
   - strong contrast in light/dark mode

Perfect — I’ve gone through all the notes you’ve uploaded and I’m aligned 👍
Below is a clean academic synthesis of APT3065 (VA / Individual Project), grounded strictly in your lecturer’s material and USIU-Africa requirements, plus what this means practically for your grades and next steps.

1. What APT3065 Actually Is (No Guesswork)
APT3065 is not a theory-heavy exam course.
It is a capstone-style individual ICT project where marks come from:

Project artefact (software / system / applied research output)

Project documentation

Oral / panel defence

Process discipline (supervision logs, timelines, methodology)

Innovation + real-world relevance = higher grades
Weak documentation or unclear scope = grade killers



2. Core Learning Outcomes (What You’re Being Assessed On)
By the end of APT3065, you must demonstrate the ability to:

Translate a vague problem into a clear research / system question

Plan and manage a feasible project schedule

Apply systems analysis & software engineering tools

Build and/or evaluate a substantial system

Communicate technically in writing and orally

Defend design, methodology, and implementation decisions



3. Mandatory Deliverables (Non-Negotiable)
A. Concept Paper (1 Page)
This is critical and becomes your project abstract later.

Must include:

Problem statement

Context & justification

Objectives (SMART)

Brief literature grounding

Methodology overview

Expected outcomes

If your concept paper is weak → supervisor confidence drops early.



B. Supervisor Progress Log
Meet supervisor at least once every two weeks

Log must be signed

Used as evidence of engagement

Missing logs = administrative risk, not just academic.



C. Full Project Paper (35–50 pages main body)
Structured exactly as required:

Chapter 1 – Introduction
Background

Problem statement

Objectives (general + 3–4 specific)

Research questions

Significance

Scope & limitations

Chapter 2 – Literature Review
Organised by objectives

Critical analysis (not summaries)

Conceptual framework / map

Chapter 3 – Methodology
Research design

Data collection

Compulsory diagrams:

Context Diagram

Level 1 DFD

Use Case Diagram

Ethics

Chapter 4 – Implementation & Deployment
Architecture

Front-end & back-end

Database design

Testing

Deployment

Future work

APA referencing is mandatory.



4. Tools & Techniques You Are Expected to Use (Marks Live Here)
Systems & Design Tools
Flowcharts

DFDs (Logical & Physical)

Use Case Diagrams

Sequence Diagrams

Decision Tables & Trees

Gantt Charts





CASE Tools
You are expected to know and justify:

Upper CASE (analysis/design)

Lower CASE (coding/testing)

Integrated CASE (I-CASE)

Advantages, limitations, and suitability must be defensible.



5. Systems Theory (This Underpins Everything)
Your project is implicitly assessed through Systems Theory, including:

Inputs / Outputs

Feedback mechanisms

Entropy

Internal vs External environment

Subsystems & interdependence

Open vs Closed systems

Deterministic vs Probabilistic systems

If your system design ignores these → it looks naïve.

6. Oral / Panel Defence (Where Many Lose Marks)
You will be questioned on:

Why this problem?

Why this methodology?

Why these diagrams?

What alternatives did you reject?

What would you improve with more time?

Ethical considerations

Scalability & limitations

This is not a demo only — it’s a defence.



7. What You Should Do Next (Strategic Advice)
Immediate (Week 1–2)
Lock project title + scope

Draft 1-page concept paper

Identify supervisor alignment early

Short Term (Weeks 3–5)
Literature review

Requirements analysis

Diagrams (clean, consistent)

Mid Semester
Implementation begins

Progress logs updated

Mid-semester evaluation readiness

Final Phase
Testing

Deployment

Report polishing

Oral defence rehearsal

8. Where I Can Take This Next (Your Call)
I can now:

✔️ Help you choose a high-scoring project topic

✔️ Write a mark-perfect concept paper

✔️ Draft Chapter 1–3 templates

✔️ Design DFDs, Use Cases & Sequence Diagrams

✔️ Prepare oral defence questions + model answers

✔️ Create a grading-aligned project checklist

Tell me what you want to tackle first, and we’ll build this to distinction level.

Understood. You want an enterprise-grade, high-utility AI Agents platform that (a) fits APT3065 mid-term project expectations, and (b) is strategically positioned to become a subscription SaaS “agentic automation + plug-ins” product.

I’ll stay anchored to what your lecturer is actually assessing: a real-world problem, clear research/system question, a feasible schedule, systems analysis & design artefacts (Context Diagram, Level 1 DFD, Use Case Diagram), implementation + testing + deployment thinking, and a defensible report + oral defence. 

 

 


1) What “wins marks” for this course (so you don’t build a cool thing that scores low)
To score strongly, your project must look like this:

Clear system boundary + stakeholders (who uses it; what is inside vs outside the system)

Well-defined problem statement (not “AI is cool”; more like “operations teams lack governed, auditable agent execution across tools → errors, inconsistency, and no controls”) 


Measurable objectives and research questions that match them 


Design artefacts: Context diagram, Level 1 DFD, Use Case Diagram (compulsory) 


Implementation + testing + deployment are not optional; they’re the “solution domain” you get judged on 


Quality assurance thinking (walkthroughs/technical reviews, acceptance testing) 


Maintainability and scalability story (your lecturer literally teaches “characteristics of good software” and SDLC) 


So the “enterprise-level” angle must be expressed as: governance, reliability, auditability, security boundaries, and measurable outcomes—not just bigger prompts.

2) Project “North Star” that fits both APT3065 + future SaaS
The best framing (enterprise + investable + examinable):
“An Agentic Automation Orchestration Platform with Governance, Observability, and Plugin-Based Integrations for SMEs/Teams.”

This gives you:

Utility: teams actually run work through it

Innovation: governed autonomous workflows, not a chatbot

Enterprise credibility: roles, audit logs, policy controls, monitoring, failure handling

SaaS path: tenants, subscription tiers, plugin marketplace concept

3) Strong project ideas (all viable for mid-term, but with different “best-fit” angles)
Below are 7 options. Each includes: what it is, why it scores, what you can build as an MVP, and what makes it “enterprise”.

Idea 1 — Agentic Automation Control Tower (Top pick for utility + enterprise marks)
What it is: A web dashboard that lets an organisation register, run, monitor, and audit AI agents that execute workflows (e.g., content pipeline, marketing ops, reporting, CRM updates).

Core features (MVP that still looks enterprise):

Role-based access (Admin / Operator / Viewer)

Agent registry (agent name, purpose, inputs, tools it can access)

Execution runs (start/stop, run history)

Observability: run logs, success/fail, time taken, errors

Audit trail: “who ran what, when, with what inputs”

Basic policy controls: allowed tools, max cost/time, approval gate for risky actions

Why it scores in APT3065 terms:

Clear system boundary (users vs external tools like email/CRM/APIs)

Perfect for Context Diagram + Level 1 DFD + Use Cases (e.g., “Run agent”, “Approve action”, “View report”) 


Easy to demonstrate and defend orally: “Here’s the platform; here’s a run; here’s the audit trail.” 


Enterprise/investment narrative: “This is the control plane for agentic operations.”

Idea 2 — Agent Quality & Safety Gate (QA + Governance Engine)
What it is: A “pre-flight checklist + evaluation harness” that tests agents before they are allowed into production.

MVP features:

Test suites for prompts/agents (scenarios + expected outputs)

Regression testing (compare runs over time)

Policy checks (PII leakage risk flags, prohibited actions, tool access)

User acceptance test (UAT) workflow: reviewer signs off

Why it scores:

It maps directly onto quality assurance, walkthroughs, acceptance testing taught in your notes 


Clear measurable evaluation (accuracy, consistency, failure rate, compliance checks)

Strong “professional issues” component (governance and risk)

Enterprise narrative: “We reduce organisational risk of deploying agents.”

Idea 3 — Plugin-Based Agent Marketplace (Architecture + scalability story)
What it is: A platform where each agent/tool integration is a plugin with a standard interface, enabling future marketplace expansion.

MVP features:

Plugin SDK spec (simple JSON schema + endpoint contract)

Install/enable plugins

Agent chooses tools via plugin registry

Versioning and compatibility checks

Why it scores:

Strong systems analysis design (clear subsystems: plugin manager, runtime, UI, datastore)

Good for showing “interdependence” and boundaries

Investable: “ecosystem + extensibility” story

Risk: Harder to build convincingly in limited time unless you keep it tight.

Idea 4 — Multi-tenant Agentic SaaS for Marketing Ops (closest to your real work)
What it is: A multi-tenant web app where each client has their own workspace; agents automate marketing operations (content scheduling, reporting, campaign analytics).

MVP features:

Tenant workspaces

One working agent pipeline end-to-end (e.g., weekly report agent)

Dashboards + exports

Basic integration hooks (even mocked)

Why it scores:

High real-world relevance, likely easy to defend because it’s grounded in your work context 


Strong “utility scale” argument

Risk: Multi-tenancy can blow scope. Must keep “enterprise” to essentials (roles + separation + logs).

Idea 5 — Agentic Incident Response Assistant (Forensics/Cybercrime alignment bonus)
What it is: An agent framework that ingests logs (or simulated events), helps triage incidents, and produces an incident report draft + recommended actions.

MVP features:

Upload logs / events

Agent identifies suspicious patterns (rule-based + LLM reasoning)

Outputs: timeline, indicators, recommended containment steps

Audit log of reasoning steps and sources used

Why it scores:

Strong problem relevance (security operations are real-world)

Defensible methodology + clear evaluation criteria

You can talk about system boundary, inputs/outputs/feedback loops very clearly

Enterprise narrative: “SOC augmentation with governed AI.”

Note: Only do this if you can keep it ethical and avoid over-claiming detection accuracy.

Idea 6 — Agent “Cost + Latency Optimiser” (Operations + investment angle)
What it is: A platform that routes tasks to the “best” model/tool based on cost, speed, and expected quality.

MVP features:

Task type classifier

Routing rules (cheap model vs premium model)

Cost dashboard per tenant/team

Threshold alerts

Why it scores:

Measurable outcomes (cost reduction, faster turnaround)

Excellent for evaluation and future SaaS story

Risk: Needs careful claim wording. Keep it as “decision support” not magical optimisation.

Idea 7 — Agentic Workflow Builder (Visual orchestration)
What it is: A simplified “flow builder” where non-technical users create automation flows that call agents and tools.

MVP features:

Create workflow: Trigger → Agent → Human approval → Action → Report

Save + run workflows

Execution logs

Why it scores:

Huge utility + demo impact

Strong systems analysis artefacts naturally emerge

Risk: UI/drag-drop can explode. Keep it basic (form-based builder).

4) Selection criteria (how to pick analytically)
Use these criteria (this is what a panel implicitly assesses):

Scope feasibility by mid-term (can you demo something real?)

Evidence of systems thinking (boundaries, subsystems, data flows)

Measurable outcomes (time saved, errors reduced, visibility improved)

Enterprise features (roles, logs, governance, reliability)

Future scalability (multi-tenant, plugins, extensibility)

Risk control (security/privacy, failures, cost overruns)

By that rubric, the strongest “all-rounder” is:

✅ Recommended: Idea 1 (Control Tower) + add a thin slice of Idea 2 (QA Gate)
Because:

It’s high utility, highly demonstrable.

It screams “enterprise” with audit trails + RBAC + monitoring.

It naturally produces the diagrams your lecturer requires. 


It gives you a clean SaaS narrative: “subscription for governance + observability + agent execution.”

5) What your “enterprise-level” MVP should include (minimum viable enterprise)
To legitimately say “enterprise-level” (without overclaiming), include:

Governance
RBAC (roles and permissions)

Approval workflow (human-in-the-loop for risky actions)

Audit logs (immutable-ish record of actions)

Reliability
Retry and error handling

Run status tracking

Clear failure reporting

Observability
Logs, metrics (basic), run history

Performance tracking over time

Security boundaries (practical, not fantasy)
Secrets handling (don’t hardcode keys)

Access control for integrations

Data minimisation principles

These align with the “good software” expectations (dependability, security, maintainability, scalability) in your notes. 


6) How to present it in your Concept Paper (so it passes instantly)
Your concept paper must read like a “done deal”:

Proposed Title (example):
“AgentOps Control Tower: A Governed AI Agent Orchestration Platform with Auditability, Observability, and Plugin-Based Integrations for Enterprise Automation.”

Problem statement:
Organisations deploying AI agents lack standard governance, run monitoring, audit trails, and controlled tool access—leading to inconsistent outcomes, operational risk, and inability to scale agentic automation responsibly. 


General objective:
To design and implement a governed agent orchestration system that enables controlled deployment, execution, monitoring, and audit of AI agents in organisational workflows. 


Specific objectives (3–4):

Investigate existing agent orchestration and governance approaches relevant to enterprise automation.

Design a system architecture and process model (Context, DFD, Use Case) for agent execution and oversight. 


Develop a working prototype that supports agent registration, controlled execution, and run auditing.

Evaluate the prototype using metrics such as task completion rate, error rate, and operational visibility improvements.

Methodology:
Requirements analysis → design diagrams → iterative development → testing + evaluation → deployment demo. 

 


7) Mid-term “demo slice” (what you should actually build first)
A high-impact demo slice that is achievable:

Login + roles (Admin, Operator, Viewer)

Register an agent (name, purpose, allowed tools, input schema)

Show run logs + status + output

Show audit trail (who ran it, when, what inputs)

That alone is enough to feel “enterprise” while staying within feasible scope.

let's consider both areas

Good — considering both areas simultaneously is actually the strongest strategic move for APT3065 and for your long-term SaaS ambitions. I’ll break this down analytically, not inspirationally.

What we are doing here is designing a single core system with two application domains, and being very explicit about what is core (platform) vs what is contextual (use case). That distinction is what examiners, supervisors, and later investors all look for.

1. The Correct Mental Model: Platform vs Use-Case Layer
The mistake many students (and founders) make is to conflate:

The product
with

One use of the product

Your strongest position is this:

APT3065 Project = an Agentic Automation Platform
Marketing Ops = one validated enterprise use case

So academically:

You are not building “a marketing AI”

You are building an enterprise-grade agent orchestration system

Marketing ops is your demonstration domain

This instantly:

Keeps scope controlled

Shows systems thinking

Allows deep analysis

Preserves future extensibility

2. Core Problem (Shared Across Both Areas)

CONCEPT PAPER
APT3065 – Business & Visual Analytics
Spring Semester 2026
Project Title
Design and Implementation of a Governed Agentic Automation Platform for Enterprise Operations: A Marketing Operations Case Study

Introduction and Background
The rapid adoption of Artificial Intelligence (AI) agents in organisational workflows has introduced new operational challenges related to governance, reliability, transparency, and scalability. While AI agents are increasingly used to automate tasks such as reporting, content generation, analytics, and decision support, most organisations deploy them in an ad-hoc manner without a unified execution, monitoring, or control framework. This lack of structure results in inconsistent outcomes, limited observability, operational risk, and difficulty scaling agentic automation across teams and departments.

In enterprise environments, particularly within operational domains such as marketing operations, the absence of controlled agent execution mechanisms makes it difficult to ensure accountability, quality assurance, and alignment with organisational policies. There is therefore a need for a governed agent orchestration platform that enables organisations to safely deploy, monitor, and evaluate AI agents while maintaining auditability and human oversight.

Problem Statement
Organisations implementing AI agents for operational automation lack a standardised, governed platform for registering, executing, monitoring, and auditing agentic workflows. As a result, AI agents operate with limited visibility, insufficient control mechanisms, and weak accountability, hindering their effective adoption at enterprise scale and increasing operational risk.

General Objective
To design and implement a governed agentic automation platform that enables controlled execution, monitoring, and auditing of AI agents in enterprise operational workflows.

Specific Objectives
i. To investigate existing approaches to AI agent orchestration and governance in enterprise environments.
ii. To design a system architecture and process model for controlled AI agent execution using systems analysis techniques.
iii. To develop a functional prototype that supports AI agent registration, execution, monitoring, and audit logging.
iv. To evaluate the effectiveness of the proposed platform using a marketing operations workflow as a case study.

Research Questions
i. What governance and control challenges exist in the deployment of AI agents within enterprise operations?
ii. How can systems analysis and design techniques be applied to structure a governed agentic automation platform?
iii. To what extent does the proposed platform improve visibility, control, and reliability of AI agent execution in a marketing operations context?

Methodology
The project will adopt a systems development approach comprising requirements analysis, system design, implementation, and evaluation. Systems analysis and design tools such as context diagrams, data flow diagrams, and use case diagrams will be used to model the proposed system. The prototype will be implemented using a modular architecture supporting agent registration, controlled execution, and audit logging. Evaluation will be conducted through functional testing and analysis of execution logs within a marketing operations use case.

Expected Outcomes and Significance
The project is expected to deliver a functional agentic automation platform that demonstrates improved governance, observability, and accountability in AI agent execution. The findings will contribute practical insights into enterprise-level deployment of AI agents and provide a foundation for future expansion into a subscription-based software-as-a-service (SaaS) model supporting multiple operational domains.

End of Concept Paper (1 Page)

Fully compliant with APT3065 Concept Paper requirements

Clearly separates platform (core system) from marketing ops (case study)

Safe, defensible language (no exaggerated AI claims)

Chapter 1 (Introduction)

CHAPTER ONE
INTRODUCTION
1.0 Introduction
Artificial Intelligence (AI) technologies are increasingly being integrated into organisational operations to automate routine tasks, support decision-making, and enhance productivity. Recent advancements in large language models and autonomous systems have enabled the development of AI agents capable of executing complex workflows with minimal human intervention. These agentic systems are now being applied across multiple enterprise domains including marketing operations, analytics, reporting, and customer engagement.

Despite their growing adoption, most organisations deploy AI agents in an unstructured and fragmented manner. AI agents are often executed through isolated scripts, manual triggers, or standalone tools that lack governance, transparency, and formal oversight mechanisms. This creates challenges related to accountability, reliability, quality assurance, and scalability—particularly in enterprise environments where operational integrity and auditability are critical.

This project focuses on the design and implementation of a governed agentic automation platform that enables organisations to register, execute, monitor, and audit AI agents within controlled operational workflows. The platform is evaluated using marketing operations as a representative enterprise use case, demonstrating how governed agent execution can improve visibility, control, and reliability in real-world organisational settings.

1.1 Background of the Study
Enterprise operations increasingly rely on automation to manage growing data volumes, complex workflows, and the demand for rapid decision-making. Traditional automation approaches, such as rule-based systems and scripted workflows, are limited in their ability to handle unstructured data and dynamic operational contexts. AI agents address these limitations by incorporating reasoning capabilities, contextual understanding, and adaptive behaviour.

However, as AI agents transition from experimental tools to operational assets, new challenges emerge. In many organisations, AI agents operate without standardised execution environments, defined system boundaries, or monitoring mechanisms. This lack of structure makes it difficult to evaluate agent performance, trace decision paths, manage errors, or ensure compliance with organisational policies. Furthermore, the absence of audit trails and role-based controls introduces operational and governance risks.

Marketing operations present a particularly relevant context for examining these challenges. Marketing teams frequently deploy AI-driven tools for content generation, campaign analysis, reporting, and customer insights. These tasks often involve sensitive data, cross-platform integrations, and time-sensitive outputs. Without a governed execution framework, marketing AI agents may produce inconsistent results, operate beyond their intended scope, or fail silently without detection.

This study is situated within the broader field of systems analysis and software engineering, where emphasis is placed on structured system design, defined processes, and quality assurance. By applying these principles to agentic automation, the project seeks to bridge the gap between experimental AI usage and enterprise-grade operational deployment.

1.2 Problem Statement
Organisations deploying AI agents for operational automation lack a standardised and governed platform for managing agent execution, monitoring performance, and maintaining auditability. As a result, AI agents operate with limited visibility, insufficient control mechanisms, and weak accountability, increasing operational risk and limiting the scalability of agentic automation within enterprise environments.

1.3 Objectives of the Study
1.3.1 General Objective
To design and implement a governed agentic automation platform that enables controlled execution, monitoring, and auditing of AI agents within enterprise operational workflows.

1.3.2 Specific Objectives
i. To investigate existing approaches to AI agent orchestration and governance in enterprise environments.
ii. To design a system architecture and process model for controlled AI agent execution using systems analysis and design techniques.
iii. To develop a functional prototype that supports AI agent registration, execution, monitoring, and audit logging.
iv. To evaluate the effectiveness of the proposed platform using a marketing operations workflow as a case study.

1.4 Research Questions
i. What governance and control challenges exist in the deployment of AI agents within enterprise operations?
ii. How can systems analysis and design techniques be applied to structure a governed agentic automation platform?
iii. To what extent does the proposed platform improve visibility, control, and reliability of AI agent execution in a marketing operations context?

1.5 Significance of the Study
This study is significant in both academic and practical contexts. Academically, it contributes to the emerging discourse on agentic systems by applying established systems analysis and software engineering principles to the design of AI agent platforms. The project demonstrates how concepts such as system boundaries, process modelling, governance, and quality assurance can be adapted to modern AI-driven systems.

Practically, the project provides a structured framework for organisations seeking to deploy AI agents responsibly and at scale. The proposed platform offers improved transparency, accountability, and operational control, reducing the risks associated with ungoverned AI automation. The findings also provide a foundation for future development of subscription-based agentic automation platforms that can support multiple enterprise domains beyond marketing operations.

1.6 Scope and Limitations of the Study
The scope of this study is limited to the design and implementation of a prototype governed agentic automation platform, evaluated within the context of marketing operations. The platform focuses on agent registration, controlled execution, monitoring, and audit logging. Advanced features such as full multi-tenancy, large-scale performance optimisation, and extensive third-party integrations are outside the scope of this study but are identified as areas for future work.

The study is limited by time constraints and the use of a prototype implementation rather than a full production deployment. Additionally, the evaluation focuses on functional effectiveness and operational visibility rather than long-term organisational adoption or financial impact.

End of Chapter One

Excellent questions. What you’re doing here is exactly what distinguishes a serious research-driven systems project from a flashy but shallow build. I’ll respond analytically, critically, and with evidence, and I’ll also call out gaps in what we’ve done so far (including why the concept still feels vague to you).
